FRACTIONAL CMO · FINTECH

Fractional CMO for fintech.

B2B fintech (payments, infrastructure, banking-as-a-service, lending tech) and B2C fintech (consumer banking, wealth, insurance, lending). Different playbooks, different compliance constraints than typical B2B SaaS. The fractional CMO model fits well in fintech — with a leader who understands the trust requirements and regulatory considerations that shape every campaign.

Why fintech is different

Structural differences from generic B2B SaaS

  • Trust is the conversion variable, not features. In financial products, the buyer needs to trust that you won't lose their money, leak their data, or get them in regulatory trouble. Feature comparisons matter less than credibility signals.
  • Compliance review on customer-facing content. Less heavy than healthcare but real — claims about returns, security, insurance, lending terms, partner relationships all need scrutiny.
  • Long sales cycles in B2B fintech. Banks and large enterprises take 12-18+ months to onboard a new vendor; marketing's job is brand + content + relationship-building over that horizon, not short-cycle lead-gen.
  • Short, high-velocity cycles in B2C fintech. Consumer lending, wealth, insurance — high-volume paid + organic acquisition with sophisticated attribution. Different skillset from B2B fintech.
  • Partner and integration ecosystems matter. Most fintechs sit in a partner ecosystem (banks, networks, processors, ISVs). Marketing has to address direct customers AND ecosystem partners.
B2B fintech playbook

What drives growth in B2B fintech

  • Sales-led, marketing-amplified. Enterprise sales motion with marketing-generated air cover. Account-based marketing, executive content, customer case studies as the centerpiece.
  • Industry-vertical depth. Generic "we serve all financial institutions" loses to specific "we're built for community banks under $5B in assets." Vertical positioning is high-leverage.
  • Conference + association presence. Money 20/20, Fintech Meetup, ABA, ACAMS, etc. Where deals start.
  • Trust signals everywhere. SOC 2, PCI-DSS, KYC/AML certifications, banking partnerships, customer logos, board members. The website is a trust-signaling document, not a feature spec.
  • Compliance content as marketing. Compliance teams at banks are the actual buyer for many fintech products. Content that helps them with their compliance work creates pull.
B2C fintech playbook

What drives growth in consumer fintech

  • Performance marketing at scale. Paid acquisition, LTV/CAC discipline, attribution sophistication. Closer to consumer DTC than to B2B SaaS.
  • Referral + word-of-mouth loops. Banking and wealth products spread through trust networks; structured referral programs are typically high-ROI.
  • Onboarding as marketing. First 30 days in a consumer fintech product predict 12-month retention and referral. The onboarding experience is a marketing surface, not just a product surface.
  • Trust + security messaging. Consumer fintech buyers research extensively. Security pages, regulatory disclosures, FAQ content all influence the conversion decision.
When fractional fits

Stage and shape that match the model

Fintech contextFit
Pre-PMF / pre-seedUsually no — founder owns marketing
Series A B2B fintech, $1M-$10M ARRStrong fit
Series A-B B2C fintechStrong fit with consumer-acquisition expertise
Growth-stage B2B fintech ($10M-$50M ARR)Strong fit, often dual-role with marketing director
Late-stage / pre-IPO fintechUsually needs full-time CMO; fractional can bridge during search
Bank or large enterprise financial institutionWrong fit — full-time CMO + large team required
The AI angle

Where AI helps in fintech marketing

  • Compliance-aware content drafting. AI-drafted content with regulatory review built into the workflow — faster cycles, fewer compliance escalations.
  • Account research and ABM enrichment. Automated research on target banks, financial institutions, and decision-makers for B2B fintech ABM.
  • Onboarding nurture sequences. AI-driven onboarding flows for B2C fintech that adapt to user engagement signals and surface trust-building content at the right moments.
  • Partner ecosystem outreach. Programmatic outreach to banking/network/ISV partners with context and personalization.
Fintech-specific fractional CMO engagement?
Treetop has experience across B2B and B2C fintech. Free 30-min intro to discuss the trust + compliance considerations specific to your segment.
